title: Netanyahu to stop attacking Iranian gas fields after Trump's request | ABC NEWS
description: Israel has agreed to pause attacks on Iranian gas fields after a request from US President Donald Trump. Trump said the US was not involved in the attack. title: Labor explores new gas tax to shield from Iran war shock | ABC NEWS
description: Anthony Albanese's department has requested Treasury modelling on options to impose a new levy on gas companies, as well as further changes to the Petroleum Resources Rent Tax.  The document, seen by the ABC, suggests the government is seeking advice on whether to impose the tax in the May budget to collect some of the windfall profits gas companies are making during the Middle East war. title: Pakistan and Afghanistan declare temporary ceasefire for Eid | Asia News Week | ABC NEWS
description: 0:00 Pakistan, Afghanistan to pause fighting for Eid, as dispute rages over Kabul bombing target 0:46 What are the conditions of ceasefire? 1:46 What is Pakistan and Afghanistan saying about the Kabul hospital strike? 2:46 Why is Afghanistan and Pakistan fighting?  Pakistan and Afghanistan said they were pausing their military operations against each other on Wednesday for the Islamic festival of Eid-al-Fitr, in a surprise move two days after a drug rehab centre in Kabul was hit in the deadliest strike in months. South Asia Bureau Chief Meghna Bali tells Asia News Week's Yvonne Yong how the temporary ceasefire came about and explains why these neighbouring countries have been fighting. title: Market value of Australian stocks down $260b since Middle East war | Finance Report | ABC NEWS
description: Worries about a prolonged Middle East war, higher inflation, and interest rate hikes have erased about $260 billion from the Australian share market in the past three weeks. Amid these concerns, the Trump administration will consider easing sanctions on stranded Iranian oil in a desperate bid to keep prices down, as finance presenter David Chau explains. title: Journalist and crew survive Israeli attack claimed to be targeted | ABC NEWS
description: A news crew has narrowly avoided being hit by an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon that landed metres from where they were broadcasting.  Steve Sweeney, the Lebanon bureau chief for RT News — an English-language Russian state broadcaster — said he and his camera operator "miraculously survived" the attack and were treated in hospital.  "This was a DELIBERATE TARGETED ATTACK on journalists, there's no mistake about it. This was an Israeli precision strike FROM A FIGHTER JET," RT News wrote on X.  Sweeney was broadcasting from the Qasmiyeh crossing in southern Lebanon, wearing a protective vest displaying the word "PRESS".  Sweeney said the strike left him with a shrapnel "deeply embedded" in his left arm, which had to be removed in hospital. title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle approaches the NT and threatens to bring another major flood | ABC NEWS
description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is due to hit the Northern Territory’s east coast, with Groote Eylandt feeling the impacts as early as Saturday afternoon. Preparation has seen the military called in with hundreds of people evacuated from Arnhem Land communities and flown to Darwin for shelter. In Katherine the town is bracing for more widespread flooding just weeks after being devastated by its biggest flood in 30 years. By James Elton for ABC News Northern Territory. title: Melbourne Storm’s Waka Program Changing Young Lives | The Pacific | ABC News
description: In Melbourne’s Pasifika community, the Storm Waka program is becoming a lifeline for at-risk youth. The program is run during school holidays, a time when young Pasifika teens may otherwise slip into risky behaviour.
